purpose.summary: The COBOL program PAUDBLOD interacts with an IMS database to insert child segments. It retrieves a root segment and then attempts to insert a child segment related to that root. The program includes error handling and file closing routines.

called_programs.purpose: To perform IMS database operations, specifically to retrieve (GU) and insert (ISRT) segments.
To perform IMS database operations, specifically to insert (ISRT) a child segment.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph attempts to retrieve a root segment from the IMS database and then calls another paragraph to insert a child segment. It initializes PAUT-PCB-STATUS (line 5) and then calls CBLTDLI with FUNC-GU to retrieve the root segment using the PAUTBPCB, PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY, and ROOT-QUAL-SSA (lines 6-9). It checks the PAUT-PCB-STATUS after the call (line 15). If the status is spaces, it assumes the retrieval was successful and calls 3200-INSERT-IMS-CALL to insert the child segment (line 19). If the status is not spaces or 'II', it displays an error message and calls 9999-ABEND (lines 20-23). The paragraph uses DISPLAY statements for debugging (lines 4, 10, 16, 21, 22).

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 3100-INSERT-CHILD-SEG paragraph. It contains a simple EXIT statement to allow the PERFORM THRU structure in 3100-INSERT-CHILD-SEG to function correctly. It does not perform any specific logic or data manipulation.

This paragraph attempts to insert a child segment into the IMS database. It calls CBLTDLI with FUNC-ISRT to insert the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS segment using the PAUTBPCB and CHILD-UNQUAL-SSA (lines 34-37). It checks the PAUT-PCB-STATUS after the call (line 39). If the status is spaces, it displays a success message (line 40). If the status is 'II', it displays a message indicating the segment already exists (line 43). If the status is neither spaces nor 'II', it displays an error message and calls 9999-ABEND (lines 45-48). The paragraph uses DISPLAY statements for debugging (lines 33, 40, 43, 46, 47).

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 3200-INSERT-IMS-CALL paragraph. It contains a simple EXIT statement to allow the PERFORM THRU structure in 3100-INSERT-CHILD-SEG to function correctly. It does not perform any specific logic or data manipulation.

This paragraph closes two input files, INFILE1 and INFILE2. It displays a message indicating that it is closing the files (line 57). It closes INFILE1 (line 58) and checks the WS-INFIL1-STATUS. If the status is spaces or '00', it continues; otherwise, it displays an error message with the file status (lines 60-64). It then closes INFILE2 (line 65) and performs a similar status check using WS-INFIL2-STATUS (lines 67-71).

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 4000-FILE-CLOSE paragraph. It contains a simple EXIT statement to allow the PERFORM structure to function correctly. It does not perform any specific logic or data manipulation.

This paragraph handles abnormal termination of the program. It displays a message indicating that the IMS load is abending (line 81). It sets the RETURN-CODE to 16 (line 83) and then executes a GOBACK statement to terminate the program (line 84).

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 9999-ABEND paragraph. It contains a simple EXIT statement. However, given that 9999-ABEND performs a GOBACK, this paragraph is likely never reached in practice.

error_handling.condition: PAUT-PCB-STATUS NOT EQUAL TO SPACES AND 'II' after root segment retrieval
PAUT-PCB-STATUS NOT EQUAL TO SPACES AND 'II' after child segment insertion
WS-INFIL1-STATUS not equal to spaces or '00' during file close
WS-INFIL2-STATUS not equal to spaces or '00' during file close
